publications.title: World Risk Poll 2026 report: Alone together: The hidden consensus on climate change
publications.description: This report explores public perceptions of the threat of climate change - perceptions that are shaped by people’s lived experience of risk in their daily lives as much as by scientific evidence.

publications.title: Asymmetric economic impacts of climate extremes on Italian agriculture: a nonlinear ARDL approach
publications.description: This study investigates the asymmetric economic impacts of climate extremes on the Italian agricultural sector.

publications.title: Country perspectives on pre-arranged financing: Belize, Grenada and Jamaica
publications.description: This report presents a comparative synthesis of how stakeholders in three Caribbean Small Island Developing States — Belize, Grenada, and Jamaica — value the core attributes of pre-arranged financing (PAF) for sovereign disaster risk.

publications.title: Country perspectives on pre-arranged financing: Kenya, Malawi, Nigeria, Senegal and Zambia
publications.description: This report presents the first cross-country study to use a structured simulation to elicit how policymakers in five African countries value the core attributes of pre-arranged financing (PAF) systems for disaster response.

publications.title: Women and climate adaptation in rural sub-Saharan Africa: constraints and research priorities
publications.description: This brief reviews the empirical evidence on the barriers women face in adapting to climate change in rural Sub-Saharan Africa. A

publications.title: What governments want from pre-arranged financing: evidence from Africa and the Caribbean
publications.description: This policy brief presents the first cross-country study of what governments want from pre-arranged financing (PAF) — funding secured in advance to respond to disasters.

publications.title: Where heat does not come in waves: a framework for understanding and managing chronic heat
publications.description: This study argues that heat research and practice have focused too narrowly on heat waves as acute, episodic emergencies, obscuring the chronic exposure of billions of people to dangerous levels of heat for months at a time.

publications.title: Competing in the face of climate risks: evidence from firms and policy priorities in MENAAP
publications.description: This World Bank Development Report examines how rising climate risks are reshaping the competitiveness of firms across the Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan, and Pakistan (MENAAP) region.

publications.title: Building a safer society through science and technology: a compendium of Korea’s science and technology-based disaster management
publications.description: This casebook highlights the implementation of science and technology-based disaster management, featuring 24 Korean practices across four thematic areas, along with Korea's success factors and remaining challenges. It is available in English and Korean.

publications.title: Financing heat resilience
publications.description: This report explores how governments can finance heat resilience through risk-informed investments, innovative financing mechanisms, and resilient infrastructure to protect lives, livelihoods, and economies from rising extreme heat risks.

publications.title: 24th Africa Working Group on Disaster Risk Reduction
publications.description: This outcome document of the 24th Africa Working Group on Disaster Risk Reduction.

publications.title: Barbados: Strengthening disaster resilience of MSMEs through business continuity planning: Guidance and sectoral templates
publications.description: This document provides guidance for Micro, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (MSMEs) in Barbados to develop a basic Business Continuity Plan, eventually aligning with the highest international standards. It includes 5 sectoral templates.

publications.title: A framework towards assessing climate change and disaster-related losses of biodiversity and ecosystem services (FRAME-ECO)
publications.description: This report introduces a framework for assessing climate change and disaster-related losses of biodiversity and ecosystem services.

publications.title: Strengthening the disaster resilience of SMEs in Sendai
publications.description: This landscape study assesses how SMEs in Sendai city  perceive climate change and disaster risks and how they assess the resilience of their companies and the cities in which they operate.

publications.title: UNDRR Annual Report 2025
publications.description: The UNDRR Annual Report 2025 presents several important achievements that UNDRR marked in the year 2025

publications.title: When digital systems fail: An expert report on the hidden risks of our digital world
publications.description: This expert report examines how digital infrastructure, now central to essential services such as healthcare, finance, and emergency response, is creating new forms of systemic risk through increasing interdependence.

publications.title: UNDRR Regional Office for Africa Quarterly Newsletter (Jan-Mar 2026)
publications.description: UNDRR's Regional Office for Africa has published its first quarterly newsletter of 2026, detailing the major achievements of the work being done by the organisation.

publications.title: Teaching resilience with the Stop Disasters game: a teacher's guide
publications.description: This Teacher’s Pack supports the use of the  Stop Disasters online game as a practical way to introduce disaster risk reduction (DRR) and resilience in the classroom.
